name: SignalR hub negotiate reachable by anonymous callers
description: POSTs to a SignalR hub's negotiate endpoint (/hubs/<name>/negotiate) with no credentials. A hub mapped without an authorization policy answers the handshake for anyone, returning a connectionId / connectionToken and the availableTransports list — the entry point to opening a live hub connection and invoking whatever methods the hub exposes. In production this means the real-time surface (and every hub method's implicit trust in the caller) is reachable pre-auth. The probe only proves the negotiate handshake is open; whether individual hub methods are themselves authorized is a separate check, but an open negotiate is the necessary first step and a strong misconfiguration signal on its own.

vulnerability.remediation: Gate the hub behind an authorization policy so negotiate rejects anonymous callers: annotate the hub class with `[Authorize]` (or map it with `app.MapHub<T>("/hubs/probe").RequireAuthorization()`), and validate the access token SignalR carries on the negotiate + connection requests (`options.AccessTokenProvider`). Verify with `curl -k -X POST https://host/hubs/<name>/negotiate` — an authorized hub returns 401, not a connectionToken. If the hub is intentionally public, keep every hub method individually `[Authorize]`d and accept this finding explicitly.
